question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

events.title: When will Bitcoin hit $150k?
events.description: When will Bitcoin hit $150k

question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a full U.S. ground invasion of Iran before 2027 at under 20%, reflecting de-escalation signals after February 2026 U.S.-Israeli airstrikes and naval blockade of Iranian ports amid Strait of Hormuz clashes. President Trump announced termination of major combat operations before the 60-day War Powers Resolution deadline on May 1, with House Speaker Mike Johnson affirming the U.S. is not at war. Recent diplomatic proposals focus on reopening Hormuz shipping, nuclear curbs, and sanctions relief, as the U.S. Navy escorts commercial vessels through the strait. Absent official ground troop deployments or escalatory triggers like regime collapse, traders weigh high invasion risks—logistical challenges, Iranian retaliation, and oil market disruptions—against historical U.S. restraint in limited strikes. Ongoing talks in Pakistan could further ease tensions before year-end.

question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 15, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, extended multiple times since April 2026 amid a brief war involving airstrikes and Strait of Hormuz disruptions, remains in effect as Pakistan-mediated talks in Islamabad stall over core issues like nuclear enrichment limits, ballistic missile curbs, full sanctions relief, and regional security guarantees. Iran recently submitted a 14-point peace proposal demanding a 30-day war end and reconstruction aid, while softening its stance to include nuclear discussions, but President Trump has rejected key terms and threatened escalated attacks if no progress occurs. Diplomats report proximity to a one-page memorandum for de-escalation and future negotiations, though verification and enforcement gaps persist. Traders weigh low odds for a permanent deal soon against historical negotiation patterns and unresolved disputes, with potential high-level meetings looming in the next week.

question: Iran closes its airspace by May 8?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran initiates a major closure of its airspace, that is not solely due to weather conditions, by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A “major closure” is defined as a broad closure, cancellation, or suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian Airspace region. A qualifying closure must apply generally to flights across Iran or a major Iranian airspace region. Limited cancellations, delays, or other partial closures will not qualify. Limited exceptions to a broad closure, however, will not disqualify such a closure from counting (e.g. exceptions for certain flights pre-approved by the Iranian Civil Aviation Authority may be permitted). 

Any non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals and departures affecting at least two of the following airports will qualify: Imam Khomeini International Airport (IKA), Mehrabad Airport (THR), Mashhad International Airport (MHD), Shiraz International Airport (SYZ), or Isfahan International Airport (IFN).

Qualifying previous examples include the January 2026 total closure of Iranian airspace except to international flights with express governmental permission (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iran-shuts-airspace-most-flights-flightradar24-says-2026-01-14/) and the April 2024 closure of Iran’s western airspace (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iranian-airports-cancel-flights-until-monday-morning-2024-04-14/). Non-qualifying examples include the January 27, 2026 partial closure of Iranian airspace around the Strait of Hormuz (https://www.aa.com.tr/en/middle-east/iran-issues-airspace-notice-for-military-drills-near-strait-of-hormuz/3812508) and the January 25 - April 25, 2026 VFR (Visual Flight Rules) flights suspension which did not broadly close commercial aviation in a qualifying region (https://www.intellinews.com/iran-suspends-general-aviation-and-vfr-flights-in-new-airspace-directive-421997/).

Warnings, No-Fly-Zones, or other flight restrictions imposed by airlines or countries other than Iran will not be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution.

Airspace closures which occur solely due to weather conditions will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from Iranian aviation authorities and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Iranian ballistic missile and drone launches from Hormozgan province toward UAE energy targets on May 5—most intercepted—prompted UAE airspace restrictions and underscore fragile US-Iran ceasefire dynamics following April's truce after US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran began February 28, which triggered full regional closures through March. Iran has gradually reopened its airspace in phases since April 19, resuming commercial flights from Tehran’s Imam Khomeini International Airport on May 1, with no qualifying major broad suspension affecting key airports like IKA or Mehrabad in the past 30 days despite partial VFR bans and warnings. Traders monitor Pentagon A-10 deployments, potential Israeli retaliations, IRGC statements, Strait of Hormuz incidents, and Pakistan-mediated talks for escalation signals that could force a shutdown.

question: Will Bernie Sanders win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions California Governor Gavin Newsom as the clear frontrunner for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination at 25%, buoyed by his national profile from high-profile clashes with the Trump administration, robust fundraising, and perceived electability in swing states, as highlighted in recent Echelon Insights polling from late April showing him topping Democrats on general election viability. Former Vice President Kamala Harris trails at 9% despite a mid-April tease of another run and leads in some surveys like McLaughlin & Associates (29%), but traders weigh her 2024 loss heavily against her name recognition. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 8% draws progressive and younger voter support amid rising favorability, while Sens. Jon Ossoff and figures like Pete Buttigieg and Gov. Josh Shapiro hover lower on executive experience and battleground appeal. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm results for governors, early primary polling in Iowa and New Hampshire, and Q2 fundraising disclosures.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 15, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 93.5% implied probability for Strait of Hormuz traffic returning to normal by May 15, driven by persistent disruptions from the ongoing US-Iran conflict that slashed daily transits from a normal 120-140 vessels to under 10, often just a handful of Iran-linked ships. Despite the US launching Project Freedom on May 3 to escort commercial vessels—enabling two merchant ships to cross on May 4 under Navy protection—Iranian restrictions and threats continue to limit passage to pre-approved or regional vessels, with marine trackers showing "restricted" status as of May 5. Fresh US-Iran strikes are testing a fragile ceasefire, sustaining war-risk premiums and rerouting, with no signs of volume normalization before the deadline per IMF PortWatch data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid the US-Iran war that began February 28, Iran's Revolutionary Guards have throttled Strait of Hormuz traffic, slashing daily vessel transits from pre-crisis 120-140 to under a dozen amid military threats and a newly delineated control map. Recent US "Project Freedom" escorts enabled just two merchant ships to cross in the past week, but major carriers like Hapag-Lloyd report transits remain impossible due to escalation risks, with live trackers showing zero outbound vessels in recent hours and 68 days of disruption. Absent ceasefire talks or de-escalation signals, traders price a low likelihood of normalization—around pre-war oil flows of 20 million barrels per day—by May 31.

question: Will 40 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above the listed value for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The number of daily transit calls/arrivals includes container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a daily number of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points, made within this market’s timeframe, will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: __ ships transit the Strait of Hormuz on any day

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing U.S.-Iran hostilities since late February 2026 have decimated commercial vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz—conveying 21% of global seaborne oil trade—with IMF Portwatch logging only 191 arrivals across April, daily peaks at 5-24 amid Iranian restrictions and U.S. blockades from April 13. This chokepoint disruption has fueled a $10-15 per barrel risk premium in Brent crude futures, now hovering near $114/bbl as traders price sustained supply constraints and rerouting costs via Africa's Cape. Prediction market consensus reflects negligible implied probabilities for 40+ daily transits by April 30, pending IMF data revisions on a disputed April 27 reading. U.S. military escort operations, with initial May successes, and potential diplomatic breakthroughs loom as key catalysts for energy market volatility.

question: LoL: Team WE vs Invictus Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Team WE and Invictus Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 6 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team WE" if Team WE win the match against Invictus Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Team WE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team WE enters this LPL Split 2 Group Ascend BO3 at the bottom with a dismal 0-9 series record and just one map win in 19 games, reflecting chronic macro struggles and frequent losses to top squads like Bilibili Gaming and Top Esports. Invictus Gaming, mid-table at 4-6 overall, holds a clear edge after sweeping WE 2-0 three weeks ago in their head-to-head, bolstered by recent roster additions like top laner Breathe and mid laner JiaQi adapting to patch 26.7's engage jungler meta favoring Renard's playstyle. With top-four advancement on the line via double round-robin, WE's desperation clashes against iG's veteran core including Rookie and Meiko, though both sides show inconsistent recent form amid Group Ascend's high-stakes Fearless Draft.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and June 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for June 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 53.5% implied probability for Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic returning to normal—defined as pre-crisis levels of around 140 vessels daily—by end-June, reflecting closely contested sentiment amid the ongoing 2026 US-Iran conflict. Primary drivers include Iran's blockade since late February, slashing transits to under 10% of normal with only sporadic Iran-linked vessels passing, offset by the US Navy's May 4 launch of Project Freedom to escort commercial ships, though recent attacks on two vessels that day and surging war-risk insurance premiums have deterred uptake. Trader consensus balances diplomatic truce hopes against escalation risks, with key swing factors being weekly transit data from trackers like MarineTraffic and any US-Iran negotiation breakthroughs before June 30 resolution. Prolonged disruption threatens global oil supply chains and Brent crude pricing.

question: Iran closes its airspace by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran initiates a major closure of its airspace, that is not solely due to weather conditions, by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A “major closure” is defined as a broad closure, cancellation, or suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian Airspace region. A qualifying closure must apply generally to flights across Iran or a major Iranian airspace region. Limited cancellations, delays, or other partial closures will not qualify. Limited exceptions to a broad closure, however, will not disqualify such a closure from counting (e.g. exceptions for certain flights pre-approved by the Iranian Civil Aviation Authority may be permitted). 

Any non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals and departures affecting at least two of the following airports will qualify: Imam Khomeini International Airport (IKA), Mehrabad Airport (THR), Mashhad International Airport (MHD), Shiraz International Airport (SYZ), or Isfahan International Airport (IFN).

Qualifying previous examples include the January 2026 total closure of Iranian airspace except to international flights with express governmental permission (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iran-shuts-airspace-most-flights-flightradar24-says-2026-01-14/) and the April 2024 closure of Iran’s western airspace (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iranian-airports-cancel-flights-until-monday-morning-2024-04-14/). Non-qualifying examples include the January 27, 2026 partial closure of Iranian airspace around the Strait of Hormuz (https://www.aa.com.tr/en/middle-east/iran-issues-airspace-notice-for-military-drills-near-strait-of-hormuz/3812508) and the January 25 - April 25, 2026 VFR (Visual Flight Rules) flights suspension which did not broadly close commercial aviation in a qualifying region (https://www.intellinews.com/iran-suspends-general-aviation-and-vfr-flights-in-new-airspace-directive-421997/).

Warnings, No-Fly-Zones, or other flight restrictions imposed by airlines or countries other than Iran will not be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution.

Airspace closures which occur solely due to weather conditions will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from Iranian aviation authorities and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Iranian ballistic missile and drone launches from Hormozgan province toward UAE energy targets on May 5—most intercepted—prompted UAE airspace restrictions and underscore fragile US-Iran ceasefire dynamics following April's truce after US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran began February 28, which triggered full regional closures through March. Iran has gradually reopened its airspace in phases since April 19, resuming commercial flights from Tehran’s Imam Khomeini International Airport on May 1, with no qualifying major broad suspension affecting key airports like IKA or Mehrabad in the past 30 days despite partial VFR bans and warnings. Traders monitor Pentagon A-10 deployments, potential Israeli retaliations, IRGC statements, Strait of Hormuz incidents, and Pakistan-mediated talks for escalation signals that could force a shutdown.

eventMetadata.context_description: Top Esports enters their LPL Split 2 Group Ascend rematch against JD Gaming with a slight edge, sitting at 7-3 in series (second place tied) compared to JDG's 6-3 (fourth), in a tight race where top four advance directly to playoffs bracket stage. TES dominated their Week 2 BO3 2-0 via superior early aggression and macro, bolstered by jungler Tian's consistent playmaking and standout performances from JackeyLove. JDG responded with wins over NIP and WE but struggles against aggressive metas, starting series slowly; both employ Fearless Draft, testing adaptability. Recent TES victories over WBG and NIP underscore momentum, while JDG's midlaner HongQ provides upset potential in this double round-robin decider.

question: Will the Iranian regime fall by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 95.5% chance the Iranian regime will endure past June 30, reflecting its institutional resilience after suppressing the 2025–2026 protests that erupted amid economic crisis and escalated into nationwide unrest by January. Brutal crackdowns by loyal security forces, including the IRGC, quelled demonstrations despite thousands killed and internet blackouts, with no elite fractures or mass defections emerging even amid U.S.-Israeli airstrikes and a subsequent economic blockade halting oil exports. Recent reports show no renewed widespread protests or internal collapse signals in April–May, underscoring the regime's siege-hardened structure. Scenarios like IRGC mutiny, Supreme Leader Khamenei's incapacitation, or coordinated opposition uprising could still shift odds, though barriers remain high given historical suppression patterns.

question: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government or military officially announces or confirms that it has gained possession of any quantity of enriched uranium previously controlled by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Possession” means that the United States has actual physical custody or control of the enriched uranium, whether held within U.S. territory or elsewhere. Announcements of deals, agreements, commitments, or plans under which the United States would acquire possession of Iranian enriched uranium at a later time will not qualify.

Qualifying possession of Iranian enriched uranium may be acquired through any means, including through an agreed surrender or seizure.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that the United States has gained possession of Iranian enriched uranium will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution, even if the United States makes no formal announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a widespread consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US intelligence assessments on May 4 revealed limited damage to Iran's nuclear facilities from recent airstrikes, with the IAEA unable to account for roughly 440 kilograms of 60% enriched uranium amid reports of a relocated or missing stockpile, fostering trader skepticism toward near-term US acquisition. Iran's foreign ministry insists enriched uranium is non-transferable "sacred" material, stalling diplomacy despite a proposed one-page memorandum for halting enrichment in exchange for sanctions relief and 30-day peace talks. President Trump's public vows to secure the stockpile via US personnel contrast with failed prior efforts like reported Navy SEAL operations, while upcoming negotiations and IAEA verification could influence outcomes before May 31.

question: Will Japan win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 16.8% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, just ahead of Spain at 15.3%, as qualifiers wrapped in early April with UEFA playoffs delivering upsets like Bosnia ousting Italy on penalties and Sweden edging Poland 3-2. This bunched field underscores balanced group draws—France faces Norway's Haaland threat and Senegal in Group I, Spain meets Uruguay in Group H, England tackles Croatia in Group L—amplifying Round of 32 chaos in the expanded 48-team format where top two plus eight best third-placed teams advance. Recent power rankings highlight France's squad depth and Spain's Euro momentum, while Argentina and Brazil's aging cores temper their 8.6% shares amid uncertain Messi and post-Neymar transitions, fostering widespread upset potential across confederations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that the Iranian regime will endure past May 31, driven by its demonstrated resilience amid the 2026 US-Israel war, Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death in late February, and subsequent installation of Mojtaba Khamenei as successor on March 9. Despite widespread 2025–2026 protests, economic contraction exceeding 40% inflation, and execution sprees—including multiple death sentences issued as recently as early May—the regime has quelled unrest through IRGC crackdowns, internet blackouts, and pro-regime demonstrations, preventing institutional collapse or mass defections. Social media claims of uprisings remain unverified without evidence of territorial losses or leadership fractures. Realistic shifts could arise from large-scale IRGC mutinies, uncontrollable economic meltdown sparking nationwide revolt, or renewed foreign military escalation.
